preview_text:
The model favors Switzerland with a 77% win probability, leaving Bosnia and Herzegovina with just a 4% chance of victory. This represents a decisive gap in the forecast, with the favorite heavily preferred on a neutral ground.

The draw emerges as a meaningful possibility at 20%, suggesting the match carries real competitive texture rather than appearing entirely one-sided in outcome shape. The win probabilities diverge sharply—Switzerland's three-in-four chance versus Bosnia and Herzegovina's single-digit probability—but the 20-point spread allows room for an upset or a stalemate rather than assuming a walkover.

The model and sportsbooks diverge meaningfully. The market credits Bosnia and Herzegovina with 15% win probability, roughly four times the model's 4%, and assigns Switzerland only 62% rather than 77%—a 15-percentage-point gap. Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects true match likelihood, only that a substantial disagreement exists on the underdog's viability.
